entries.statement: ((∀ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp), (∀ x ∈ Γ, isLeftOnly x) → isLeftOnly A → (HandedSupportClosure Γ A ↔ leftTranslatedHandedSupportClosure Γ A)) ∧ (∀ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp), (∀ x ∈ Γ, isRightOnly x) → isRightOnly A → (HandedSupportClosure Γ A ↔ rightTranslatedHandedSupportClosure Γ A))) ∧ ¬((∀ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp), (∀ x ∈ Γ, isLeftOnly x) → isLeftOnly A → (SupportClosure Γ A ↔ HandedSupportClosure Γ A)) ∧ (∀ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp), (∀ x ∈ Γ, isRightOnly x) → isRightOnly A → (SupportClosure Γ A ↔ HandedSupportClosure Γ A)))
entries.docstring_summary: The exact handed invariant survives directional reductions, while the coarse/handed gap remains structural.
entries.rationale:
First restrict to left-only or right-only sequents and apply the one-sided conservativity theorem for the translated presentation.
Then replace derivability by handed support using the handed exactness theorem, obtaining preservation of the exact handed invariant across translation.
Next invoke the same-handed failure theorem to show that coarse support still does not become exact merely by restricting to one directional side.
Finally conclude that the coarse/handed separation is structural and survives standard directional reductions.
Added the u6 corollary to Scratch.lean as a packaged boundary statement: the first conjunct is exactly one-sided handed conservativity, and the second conjunct reuses the verified same-handed failure theorem to show coarse support still does not collapse to handed support on same-handed fragments. Verified with `lake env lean AutomatedTheoryConstruction/Scratch.lean`; no relevant sorry remains.
